subject: Sociology
title: Diaspora Communities
description: A micro-lesson that makes Diaspora Communities usable.

hook.text: Chinese New Year is celebrated in 120 countries. The diaspora carried culture across every ocean.

buildup.text: A diaspora is a community of people who share a homeland but live scattered across multiple countries.

discovery.text: Diasporas maintain cultural ties through food, language, religion, media, and regular contact with the homeland.

twist.text: Modern technology strengthens diaspora bonds. Video calls and social media keep transnational families connected daily.

climax.text: Diasporas challenge the idea that identity is tied to place. Belonging can span continents and generations.

punchline.text: Home is a feeling, not just a place.

quiz.question: What defines a diaspora community?
options: People sharing a homeland but living scattered across countries
